Let $q$ be a real number with $|q| \ne 1$ and let $k$ be a positive integer.
Define a Laurent polynomial $f_k (X)$ in the variable $X$, depending on $q$ and
$k$, by

$$ f_k (X) = \prod_{i=0}^{k-1} (1 - q^i X)(1 - q^{i+1} X^{-1}). $$

Show that the constant term of $f_k (X)$, i.e., the coefficient of $X^0$ in
$f_k (X)$, is equal to

$$ \frac{(1 - q^{k+1})(1 - q^{k+2}) \cdots (1 - q^{2k})}{(1 - q)(1 - q^2) \cdots (1 - q^k)}. $$
